methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Nucleus Genomics is a Series A–stage biotech company founded by Kian Sadeghi that has raised $32M to date. The valuation universe comprises 3 public comparables in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals, though none of the private peers share sufficient stage and valuation-tier overlap for direct median benchmarking.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable framework here. Using total raised ($32M) as a baseline proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $64M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue disclosure—standard for private-company reporting in this sector. Peer-median methodology is similarly inapplicable; while 8 same-sector private peers exist, none met the threshold of ≥3 comparables within the same stage, adjacent stage, and valuation tier simultaneously.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $54M (low) to $74M (high), centered on $64M. This range reflects the confidence bounds around the stage-progression estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue-based valuation anchors, limited granularity on comparable transaction multiples within the narrow peer set, and the typical opacity of Series A pricing dynamics in early-stage biotech. Any refined estimate would benefit from revenue disclosure or access to tighter peer valuations.
